name: West Coast Pawn & Gun Davenport
description_short: West Coast Pawn & Gun in Davenport, FL buys, sells, and loans against firearms, gold, electronics, and everyday items. Licensed firearms dealer and Glock stocking retailer.
description_long:
West Coast Pawn & Gun operates as a full-service pawn shop in Davenport, Florida, specializing in firearms sales and collateral-based lending. The business functions as both a retail firearms dealer and traditional pawn operation, allowing customers to either sell items outright or take short-term loans using personal property as collateral.

The shop offers three primary services: pawning everyday items (gold, firearms, electronics, tools) for quick cash; buying items of value outright; and selling new and used firearms from major manufacturers. They are an authorized Glock stocking dealer and also stock Freedom Ordnance, Smith & Wesson, DPMS, Springfield, Taurus, and Noreen Firearms. The business also offers concealed weapons training classes and FFL transfer services at $40 per transfer plus $7 for background checks. For electronics and pawned items, all products must be in good working condition and no more than 2 years old.

What distinguishes this operation is their dual focus on firearms retail and traditional pawn services. They maintain selective inventory standards—only accepting clean, working electronics under 2 years old, resetting Apple products to factory settings before sale, and requiring proper software on computers. Their FFL transfer service and custom Cerakote firearms finishing by J. Castro adds specialization beyond standard pawn offerings. They actively manage inventory visibility through Facebook updates.

As a pawn shop, West Coast Pawn & Gun serves customers needing immediate cash against collateral or looking to sell unwanted items. However, consumers should understand this is fundamentally a collateral lending business with typical pawn shop loan structures, not a personal loan or credit-building service. Their strict device age requirements and condition standards may limit what they'll accept.
pros: Authorized Glock and multi-brand firearms stocking dealer with custom Cerakote finishing available
Accepts FFL transfers from other dealers at flat $40 rate with included background checks
Offers concealed weapons training classes in-house
Clear inventory standards protect quality—requires electronics under 2 years old with no screen damage
Will source firearms not in stock if customer requests them
Serves both immediate cash needs (pawn) and permanent sales options
Active social media inventory updates on Facebook
cons: Strict age restrictions on electronics (maximum 2 years old) eliminates many common items
Limited hours of operation (closed Sunday and Monday, only 9AM-5PM weekdays)
No information provided about loan terms, interest rates, or pawn redemption periods on website
Firearms-heavy focus may limit appeal for customers seeking non-gun collateral pawn services
FFL transfer policy restricted to dealers only—cannot accept transfers from private sellers
best_for: Firearms enthusiasts and hunters seeking to buy, sell, or pawn guns or shooting accessories
Gold and jewelry sellers looking for fair assessment and immediate cash
People with recent electronics (laptops, phones, gaming systems under 2 years old) needing quick loans or sales
Concealed weapons permit holders seeking local training and firearms retail combined
diagnosis: Best for firearms owners and enthusiasts who need quick cash via collateral loans or want to buy/sell guns through a licensed dealer. Main caveat: this is a traditional pawn shop, not a credit-building service—loans require returning collateral and come with redemption deadlines; lacks transparency on rates and terms online.
services: Pawn loans on gold, firearms, electronics, and tools in good condition
Purchase and sale of firearms (handguns, rifles, shotguns, accessories)
Glock stocking dealer with new inventory
Firearms FFL transfers from licensed dealers ($40 flat fee, $7 background check)
Electronics purchase and pawn (laptops, phones, gaming systems under 2 years old)
Outright purchase of items of value with government/state ID
Sale of ammunition and firearm sights/accessories
